role_title: Product Manager, Growth AI Outreach Motion

level_signals: Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap
Own the revenue this motion produces
High-visibility role with direct accountability for business outcomes

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, taking products from strategy through to shipped outcomes
Experience building AI-powered, conversational, or agentic products. You understand what it takes to ship AI that works reliably for real users, not just in demos.
A track record of owning revenue or business outcomes through product-led approaches
Experience with hypothesis-driven product development and experimentation at scale
Ability to work across existing systems and synthesize them into a coherent product strategy rather than defaulting to net-new builds
Strong written and verbal communication, with the ability to drive alignment across a broad set of stakeholders
An ownership mindset. You do what it takes to solve problems and ship.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with growth problems (acquisition, activation, onboarding) in B2B or B2B2C environments
Hands-on familiarity with LLMs, conversational AI, or agentic architectures. You have shipped products on top of these technologies, not just evaluated them.
Experience building across channels (chat, email, voice) with an understanding of the tradeoffs between modalities
Background in scaling high-touch human experiences (sales, support, product specialists) through technology
Comfortable in high-visibility roles with direct accountability for business outcomes

scope.surface: AI-powered outreach and guidance motion for self-serve customers (inbound chat, voice, email, onboarding, cross-sell, qualification, handoff to sales)
scope.business_model: B2B and B2B2C, product-led growth for self-serve (startups and SMBs)
core_responsibilities: Define the AI specialist: what it knows, how it engages, where it draws the line, and when it hands off. Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap.
Launch and scale the inbound motion across chat, voice, and email for prospects and new users seeking help with products, pricing, and getting started.
Build the triage and qualification layer to assess users, understand opportunity size, and route accordingly.
Define escalation paths to the vendor support team and sales team.
Scale past the first interaction into onboarding and cross-sell.
Leverage Stripe's existing product recommendation, merchant context, onboarding systems, and outreach platform.
Own the revenue this motion produces and define success metrics.
Partner with engineering, AI/ML, design, data science, marketing, sales, and support.

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area with a defined mission (AI specialist at scale)
Direct revenue accountability — role has measurable business impact
Preferred qualifications explicitly called out as bonus, not requirements, lowering perceived bar
Strong cross-functional scope (engineering, AI/ML, data science, marketing, sales, support)
Stripe's scale provides significant distribution and data advantages for this product
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Preferred qualifications section has a blank leading bullet — possible formatting artifact suggesting the JD may be incomplete
High ambiguity on team size and reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement, revenue ownership, and vision/strategy ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from team description: self-serve, startups, SMBs, B2B)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics in JD)

location.type (not stated in JD; marked unclear)
